intro: Scleromitrion kerrii, also known as Thai mistflower, is a flowering plant species native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its delicate white blooms and slender foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its reported anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Scleromitrion kerrii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Scleromitrion kerrii is often prepared as a tea or infused in hot water to alleviate symptoms, or dried and made into capsules or tablets for easier consumption.
